event_description: Yes. Computer Sciences Corporation (CSC) was the subject of a major SEC accounting fraud action tied to its UK National Health Service (NHS) IT contract (part of the broader NHS National Programme for IT) and related Nordic/Denmark business accounting. CSC and several former executives (including former CEO Michael Laphen, former CFO Michael Mancuso, and other finance/controller-level executives) were accused of using improper accounting methods (e.g., manipulating estimates, hiding known problems and delays on the NHS contract, and improper revenue recognition in the Nordic segment) to inflate earnings and mislead investors from roughly 2008 through 2012. CSC restated prior-period financial statements around 2012 as a result of the Nordic/Denmark accounting issues. The SEC's investigation culminated in enforcement action, with CSC agreeing to pay roughly $190 million to settle SEC charges, and individual executives (including the former CFO and other officers) also settling related charges and/or facing officer-and-director bars. Several finance executives departed or were terminated in connection with these issues.
approx_date: Underlying misconduct ~2008-2012; restatement ~2012; SEC settlement announced June 5, 2015
